executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised in a coordinated campaign across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Kids & Company childcare, Peterborough Public Health, and Eastern Townships School Board. The Qilin ransomware group is the most active threat actor with 20+ victims, followed by CLOP (13 victims), Play (11 victims), and SafePay (10 victims). Critical infrastructure including public health, telecommunications, education, and municipal government have been breached. No CCCS advisories were issued in this period despite the scale of the campaign.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20+ Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak, A Lococo Wholesale, and Bergmanis Preyra law firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - double extortion ransomware with data leak site

items.name: CLOP
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including Strategic Objectives, Ideal Welders, Conwest, CMH Helicopters, Granite Pointe, SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Whiskijack Resorts, CCCM.BC.CA, Technikel, Modtech, Eastplats, and Centaur Products
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - known for exploiting file transfer vulnerabilities and mass exploitation campaigns

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm, Katch Kan, and Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1021 (Remote Services) - double extortion ransomware group

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 10 Canadian organizations including Briway Carriers, Matt and Steve (agriculture), Ripobec, Groupe Piche, Precision Aluminum, Kenalex, Silverline Group, Mactavish Co, and Teccart education institution
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Groupe Caddac (construction), ControlGMC (manufacturing), Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), Real Tech, and Colliers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - emerging ransomware group targeting professional services

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs, CCR Solutions, Esposito Bros Construction, Soteck (agriculture), McKee-Pownall Equine Services, and Emond Publishing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, KTL Group, and Trucash (financial services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - targets public sector and critical infrastructure

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 7 Canadian organizations including CPK Interior, Excavation Tourigny, Glassers TV, Steelworks Inc, Bison Family Medical, Omega Tool Corp, Reidhurst Nagy CPA, and Bais Yaakov education

items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations including Solutions Extreme Technology and Eeyou Communications Network (telecommunications)

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations including Active Green + Ross (consumer services), Morison Insurance Brokers, and Kids & Company (childcare services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual (business services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1133 (External Remote Services) - known for exploiting VPN vulnerabilities

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company - Database leaked (financial services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage) - confirmed database exfiltration and leak

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - targets Canadian municipal governments

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a severe ransomware epidemic with 100+ organizations breached across all provinces and sectors. Critical victims include Canada Goose (major retail brand), National Money Mart (financial services with confirmed database leak), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Eeyou Communications Network (telecom), and Municipality of North Perth. No CCCS advisories were issued despite this mass compromise event affecting public health, education, municipal government, and critical infrastructure.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company breached by Everest ransomware with database leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi; Trucash financial services compromised by Lynx; multiple accounting firms breached including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, and Reidhurst Nagy

report_2w.executive_summary: Three critical zero-day vulnerabilities are being actively exploited in the wild right now. Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (CVE-2026-20245) has no patch available and attackers are escalating privileges to root. SolarWinds Serv-U (CVE-2026-28318) is under active attack and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Everest Forms Pro plugin (CVE-2026-3300) is being exploited to take over WordPress sites via remote code execution. Additionally, password manager Dashlane disclosed a brute-force attack that resulted in encrypted vault theft, and threat actor UNC3753 is targeting US law firms with vishing, remote management tools, and physical break-ins.

items.description: Authenticated privilege escalation to root in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
Confidentiality: Low, Integrity: Low, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: None, Integrity: None, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability referenced in geopolitical context (limited detail)

items.name: UNC3753
items.targets: US law firms
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing via vishing), T1219 (Remote Access Software - RMM tools), T1200 (Hardware Additions - physical break-ins)

items.name: UNC5221
items.targets: Multiple sectors
items.ttps: T1583.003 (Acquire Infrastructure - Virtual Private Server), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), using Brickstorm and Plenet malware for persistence

items.targets: REHA-ACTIV (medical organization)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System - medical and health records)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1657 (Financial Theft - ransom payment confirmed)

items.targets: npm ecosystem developers
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise - Software Supply Chain), T1059.007 (JavaScript execution), self-spreading worm via phantom gyp packages

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 on May 29, 2026, regarding a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories. No specific Canadian retail, financial, or healthcare breaches were identified in this reporting period, though Loblaws was mentioned in an unrelated grocery context regarding frozen juice products returning to shelves.
